summary: This post shares a satirical poem by Ernie Davis responding to the revelation that Palantir secretly deployed predictive policing technology in New Orleans for six years without public knowledge. The poem draws parallels between Tolkien's palantíri (seeing stones that corrupted their users) and Peter Thiel's Palantir company, suggesting that wielding such powerful surveillance tools without wisdom leads to downfall. It traces Thiel's career from PayPal billionaire to Trump supporter, criticizing his arrogance and anti-intellectual stance. The poem recounts how political consultant James Carville brokered a secret deal to bring Palantir's predictive policing to New Orleans, bypassing city council oversight entirely. Davis argues this secrecy represents a betrayal of public trust and warns of the civil rights implications of algorithmic policing.
key_points: Palantir secretly tested predictive policing technology in New Orleans for six years, with only the mayor and police department aware
The poem draws a deliberate parallel to Tolkien's palantíri, whose power exceeded the wisdom of those who used them, leading to their corruption
Peter Thiel is portrayed as brilliant but dangerously arrogant, supporting authoritarianism while scorning education and expertise
Predictive policing circumvents the evidentiary standards of criminal justice by classifying people as vectors rather than proving specific acts
The secrecy of the arrangement, hidden even from city council, represents a fundamental betrayal of democratic accountability
money_quotes: But using a device whose might / Exceeds your wisdom risks a fearful fall.
No AI program has succeeded / In mastering the basic common sense.
But building an AI detector / For criminal propensity's no sweat. / You map a person to a vector / And classify it with a neural net.
Policing with predictive code / The guardians of security delights, / But leads us on a risky road / Toward bias and ignoring civil rights.
As long as, in the course of life, / No cousin or acquaintance goes astray.
Conclude that those who hold the reins / Of power have betrayed the public trust.
key_insight: Secret deployment of predictive policing technology, hidden from democratic oversight, represents a dangerous union of corporate arrogance and government power that threatens civil liberties.
